content:
Key Findings

  • Burnley Borough Council's spending increased by £6.2 million (10.1%) from 2021 to 2025.
  • The largest year-over-year increase was from 2022 to 2023, with a £27.6 million (50.3%) rise.
  • Spending peaked in 2024 at £87.4 million before dropping to £67.6 million in 2025.
  • The council processed 30,580 transactions across the five-year period.

Year-by-Year Spending Trends

Burnley Borough Council's spending has seen significant fluctuations over the past five years. In 2021, the council spent £61.4 million, which decreased by £6.55 million (10.7%) to £54.9 million in 2022. This decline was followed by a substantial increase of £27.6 million (50.3%) in 2023, bringing spending to £82.5 million. The upward trend continued in 2024, with a £4.9 million (6.0%) increase to £87.4 million, the highest figure in the five-year period.

The most notable change occurred between 2024 and 2025, where spending dropped by £19.77 million (22.6%) to £67.6 million. This significant decrease marks a shift from the previous years' trends and raises questions about the factors driving this change. Understanding these fluctuations is crucial for residents and policymakers to assess the council's financial management and priorities.

Analysing the Spending Peaks and Troughs

The peak spending year of 2024, at £87.4 million, suggests a period of increased investment or expenditure. This could be attributed to various factors such as infrastructure projects, social services, or other council initiatives. Conversely, the sharp decline in 2025 to £67.6 million indicates a possible reduction in spending or a shift in financial priorities. The council's decision-making during this period warrants further scrutiny to understand the underlying reasons for such a significant change.

The overall increase of £6.2 million (10.1%) from 2021 to 2025 reflects a modest growth in spending over the five-year period. However, the year-over-year variations highlight the volatility in the council's financial activities. Residents and stakeholders should be aware of these trends to engage in informed discussions about the council's budget and its impact on local services.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Burnley Borough Council about the specific projects or initiatives that led to the significant spending changes in 2023 and 2024.
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to gain insights into the council's financial decisions and budget allocations.
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see which suppliers and services received the most funding during the peak spending years.
  • Write to your local councillor to express your concerns or questions about the fluctuations in the council's spending and their impact on local services.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and conduct your own analysis of Burnley Borough Council's financial activities.
This article is based on Burnley Borough Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
